description: Docker Compose test environment + module contract tests + silent error fixes

userStories.title: Fix silent error in postSlackDm
userStories.description: As an operator, I want postSlackDm failures to be logged so I can diagnose delivery issues.
acceptanceCriteria: postSlackDm catches errors and calls log.error({ err }, 'Failed to send Slack DM') instead of swallowing them
postSlackDm accepts an optional logger argument (or uses a module-level logger fallback) so tests can verify the log call
Unit test added: postSlackDm logs error when conversations.open fails
Unit test added: postSlackDm logs error when chat.postMessage fails
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: postSlackDm is in apps/slack-relay/src/app-factory.ts. Currently returns silently when openBody.ok is false — no log emitted.

userStories.title: Add error logging throughout relay message forwarding flow
userStories.description: As an operator, I want all silent failures in the relay's message processing (Steps 4 and 5) to emit log.error so I can trace broken delivery chains.
acceptanceCriteria: Step 4 (Prisma enqueue): wrap in try/catch, log.error on failure with { err, tenantId, slackEventId }
Step 5 (forward to control plane): existing catch block already has log.warn — upgrade to log.error and include response status + body when available
Any fetch to the CP message endpoint that returns non-2xx logs the status code and response body
Unit test added: relay logs error when Prisma enqueue throws
Unit test added: relay logs error when CP message endpoint returns 500
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: processSlackEventWithConfig is in apps/slack-relay/src/app-factory.ts. Steps 4 and 5 are around lines 164-283.

userStories.title: Add contract test for relay → control plane message endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an integration test that verifies the relay correctly calls POST /v1/tenants/:tenantId/message with the expected payload and headers.
acceptanceCriteria: Test spins up a real buildSlackRelayApp instance + an in-process HTTP mock server (not a real control plane)
Mock CP server verifies: correct path /v1/tenants/<tenantId>/message, method POST, header x-relay-token present
Test sends a Slack event via POST /slack/events and asserts the mock CP received the forwarded message
Test covers the case where CP returns 200 (success path)
Test covers the case where CP returns 500 (error is logged, relay does not crash)
Test file: tests/integration/relay-to-cp-contract.test.ts
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Use existing pattern from tests/integration/control-plane.test.ts for building the app. Mock fetch or spin a real HTTP server for the CP side.

userStories.title: Create docker-compose.test.yml for full stack local testing
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a docker-compose.test.yml that brings up control-plane, slack-relay, and scheduler in isolated containers so I can test the full message delivery chain without host interference.
acceptanceCriteria: File created at docker/docker-compose.test.yml
Services: control-plane (port 3200), slack-relay (port 3101), scheduler — all built from the monorepo Dockerfile or a new multi-stage Dockerfile at docker/Dockerfile
control-plane mounts /var/run/docker.sock so it can start tenant containers
slack-relay depends_on control-plane with condition: service_healthy
control-plane has a healthcheck: curl -f http://localhost:3200/health
slack-relay has a healthcheck: curl -f http://localhost:3101/health
All services use env_file: ../../.env (relative to docker/ dir) or accept env vars via environment block with sensible defaults
A volume named tenant-data is defined and mounted at /data/tenants in control-plane
A README section or comment block in the compose file documents: how to build, how to run (docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.test.yml up --build), how to tear down
docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.test.yml config passes (valid YAML, no schema errors)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The monorepo has no root Dockerfile yet. You may need to create docker/Dockerfile as a multi-stage build (build stage: pnpm install + tsc; runtime stage: copy dist). Reference docker/tenant-image/Dockerfile for patterns. The compose file should NOT embed secrets — use env_file or runtime env vars.
